zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多

zh.Diócesis de Toledo, Spain: 西班牙迪奧希斯 德 托萊多
zh.lifeSummary: 當Teresa Alfonso de Toledo於大約1312年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，她的父親Diego Garcia de Toledo I29歲，母親Maria Garcia33歲。她於1332年與Gómez Pérez de Toledo在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Teresa Alfonso de Toledo于大约1312年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，她的父亲Diego Garcia de Toledo I29岁，母亲Maria Garcia33岁。她于1332年与Gómez Pérez de Toledo在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Teresa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Theresa .  In the English-speaking world the name is often chosen in this spelling by Roman Catholics, with particular reference to the Spanish saint, Teresa of Ávila (  Teresa Cepeda de Ahumada ,  1515–82 ).

variants.name: Theresa
meanings: Of problematic origin. The name seems to have been first used in Spain and Portugal, and, according to tradition, was the name of the wife of St Paulinus of Nola, who spent most of his life in Spain; she was said to have originated (and to have derived her name) from the Greek island of Thēra. However, this story is neither factually nor etymologically confirmed.

variants.name: Teresia
meanings: Of problematic origin. The name seems to have been first used in Spain and Portugal, and, according to tradition, was the name of the wife of St Paulinus of Nola, who spent most of his life in Spain; she was said to have originated (and to have derived her name) from the Greek island of Thēra. However, this story is neither factually nor etymologically confirmed.

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

en.lifeSummary: When Teresa Alfonso de Toledo was born about 1312, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, her father, Diego Garcia de Toledo I, was 29 and her mother, Maria Garcia, was 33. She married Gómez Pérez de Toledo in 1332, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Teresa Alfonso de Toledo est née environ 1312, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Diego Garcia de Toledo I, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Maria Garcia, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Gómez Pérez de Toledo en 1332, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Teresa Alfonso de Toledo um 1312 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Diego Garcia de Toledo I 29 und ihre Mutter Maria Garcia 33. Sie hat 1332 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien Gómez Pérez de Toledo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Teresa Alfonso de Toledo è nata il circa 1312 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, sua padre, Diego Garcia de Toledo I, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Maria Garcia, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Gómez Pérez de Toledo nel 1332, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Teresa Alfonso de Toledoが約1312年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで生まれた時，父Diego Garcia de Toledo Iは29歳，母Maria Garciaは33歳であった。1332年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでGómez Pérez de Toledoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Teresa Alfonso de Toledo(이)가 대략 1312년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Diego Garcia de Toledo I(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maria Garcia(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1332년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Gómez Pérez de Toledo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Teresa Alfonso de Toledo nasceu aproximadamente 1312, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Diego Garcia de Toledo I, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Maria Garcia, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Gómez Pérez de Toledo em 1332, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Teresa Alfonso de Toledo родилась около 1312 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, её отцу по имени Diego Garcia de Toledo I было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Garcia, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gómez Pérez de Toledo в 1332 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Teresa Alfonso de Toledo nació aproximadamente 1312, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Diego Garcia de Toledo I, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Maria Garcia, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Gómez Pérez de Toledo en 1332, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo.
